title: 10 best herbal creams for excessive sweating

remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory compounds: Herbal aloe creams contain anti-inflammatory compounds like aloin and aloe-emodin, which help reduce inflammation in the skin caused by excessive sweating, leading to a decrease in sweat production.

Antimicrobial properties: The antimicrobial agents present in herbal aloe creams, such as aloe-vera polysaccharides, inhibit the growth of bacteria that thrive in sweaty environments, reducing the risk of skin infections and unpleasant odors.

Moisturizing and soothing effects: Herbal aloe creams contain vitamins A, C, and E, as well as minerals like calcium and potassium, which help to moisturize and soothe the skin, reducing irritation and discomfort caused by excessive sweating.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Aloe vera, also known as aloe, creams helps with excessive sweating because of its unique combination of soothing and antiseptic properties. The gel extracted from the aloe plant has anti-inflammatory and antifungal properties that help to reduce irritation and inflammation associated with excessive sweating. Additionally, aloe vera creams have natural cooling properties that help to lower body temperature and provide instant relief from hot flashes and excessive sweating. This natural remedy provides effective and long-lasting results for those suffering from excessive sweating.
remedy_constituents: Aloe-emodin: This anthraquinone compound has potent anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which can help reduce sweat production and alleviate excessive sweating by preventing bacterial growth that can contribute to body odor.

Chrysophanic acid: A phenolic compound found in Aloe vera, chrysophanic acid has been shown to have a cooling effect on the skin, reducing sweat production and alleviating excessive sweating by providing a soothing and calming effect on the skin's thermoreceptors.

Ferulic acid: A phenolic acid with antioxidant properties, ferulic acid helps reduce oxidative stress and inflammation in the skin, which can contribute to excessive sweating. It also has a cooling effect that can help alleviate hot flashes and night sweats.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Aloe vera leaves are the most commonly used part in creams for excessive sweating due to their high concentration of aloe-emodin and other compounds that help reduce sweat and soothe skin irritation.
Gel from Leaves: The gel inside the leaves of Aloe vera is used in creams for excessive sweating because it contains anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties that help calm and protect the skin.

Aloin from Leaves: Aloin, a compound found in the leaves of Aloe vera, is used in creams for excessive sweating because it has a diaphoretic effect, helping to stimulate the body's natural sweating process and potentially reducing excessive sweat.
remedy_recipe: Weigh 200g of aloe vera gel and 100g of beeswax and place them in a mixing bowl.
Heat the mixture in a double boiler over low heat for 10 minutes or until the beeswax melts.
Remove the bowl from the heat and stir in 50g of coconut oil and 20g of vitamin e oil.
Add 5g of preservative and mix until it's fully dissolved in the mixture.
Pour the mixture into small containers and let it cool and solidify for 30 minutes.

remedy_properties: Tea Tree Oil's Antimicrobial Properties: Tea tree oil has been shown to reduce bacterial growth, which can contribute to excessive sweating and body odor, by killing bacteria on the skin surface.
Witch Hazel's Astringent Properties: Witch hazel is a natural astringent that helps to constrict pores and reduce sweat glands' activity, thereby reducing excessive sweating.
Lavender Oil's Calming Effects: Lavender oil has a calming effect on the nervous system, which can help to reduce stress-induced sweating by promoting relaxation and reducing anxiety.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Melaleuca alternifolia, also known as tea tree, creams helps with excessive sweating because of its antimicrobial and antifungal properties. The tea tree oil found in these creams has been shown to inhibit the growth of bacteria and fungi that can contribute to excessive sweating. By reducing the presence of these microorganisms, the cream can help to lower the body's temperature and reduce sweat production, providing relief from excessive sweating and helping to keep the skin dry and comfortable. This natural remedy offers a soothing solution for those suffering from hyperhidrosis.
remedy_constituents: Cineole (Eucalyptol): Cineole has antimicrobial and antifungal properties that help reduce bacterial and fungal growth on the skin, which can contribute to excessive sweating and body odor.
Linalool: Linalool has a cooling effect on the skin, which may help to reduce sweating by causing the blood vessels to constrict and promoting the evaporation of sweat.

Terpinen-4-ol: Terpinen-4-ol has antifungal and antibacterial properties that help to prevent the growth of microorganisms on the skin, reducing the risk of skin infections that can be exacerbated by excessive sweating.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Leaves are the primary source of tea tree oil, which is widely used due to its antifungal and antibacterial properties that help reduce sweat and odor.
Buds: Buds are also a source of tea tree oil and are used to make creams due to their antiseptic and antifungal properties that help combat excessive sweat and body odor.
Leaves are also often used in combination with other parts such as flowers to make creams as leaves are the most widely used part, but some products also contain the flowers, so:
remedy_recipe: Harvest 20-30 fresh leaves of melaleuca alternifolia and clean them thoroughly with distilled water.
Steep 10 grams of the fresh leaves in 100 milliliters of carrier oil like jojoba oil for 2 weeks.
Strain the infused oil through cheesecloth or a coffee filter to remove the plant material.
Mix 10 grams of the infused oil with 50 grams of beeswax and 50 grams of shea butter in a double boiler.
Whip the mixture until it thickens and reaches a creamy consistency after 30 minutes of cooling.

remedy_properties: Antioxidant properties: Lavender's antioxidants help reduce oxidative stress and inflammation, which can contribute to excessive sweating by soothing skin irritation and calming the nervous system.

Anti-inflammatory properties: Lavender's anti-inflammatory compounds reduce swelling and redness associated with hyperhidrosis (excessive sweating), providing relief from discomfort and promoting a sense of calm.
Calming and sedative properties: Lavender's natural anxiolytic effects help calm the nervous system, reducing stress and anxiety that can trigger or exacerbate excessive sweating.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Lavandula angustifolia, also known as English lavender, creams helps with excessive sweating because of its unique properties. The active compounds, such as linalool and linalyl acetate, have a cooling and calming effect on the skin, reducing the production of sweat. Additionally, lavender's antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties help to soothe and protect the skin, promoting a balanced and healthy sweat response. By applying lavender creams, individuals can experience relief from excessive sweating and enjoy a sense of comfort and relaxation.
remedy_constituents: Linalool: This terpene acts as a natural antiperspirant by inhibiting the activity of sweat glands, reducing excessive sweating.
Linalyl acetate: Another terpene found in lavender oil, it helps to calm the nervous system, which can contribute to excessive sweating, by reducing stress and anxiety.

Rosmarinic acid: A phenolic compound with antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, it helps to regulate body temperature and reduce sweating by modulating the activity of the sympathetic nervous system.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Rich in antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, which help soothe and calm the skin.
Flowers: Known for their antiseptic and antifungal properties, which help reduce bacterial growth and prevent infection.
Stems: Contain compounds that have been shown to reduce sweat production and improve skin moisture balance.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 100g of fresh lavandula angustifolia flowers on a sunny day in peak blooming season.
Dry the harvested flowers in a warm place with good air circulation for 7-10 days.
Steep 50g of dried lavandula angustifolia flowers in 500ml of carrier oil like coconut or jojoba oil for 2-3 weeks.
Strain the infused oil through a cheesecloth and discard the solids then mix it with 100g of beeswax and 100g of shea butter.
Heat the mixture in a double boiler or a heat-proof bowl set over a pot of simmering water until it reaches 180-190°f.

remedy_properties: Antifungal and antibacterial compounds: Maidenhair tree cream contains antifungal and antibacterial agents that help combat sweat-induced fungal and bacterial infections, reducing body odor and sweat-related issues.
Anti-inflammatory and soothing properties: The cream's anti-inflammatory and soothing properties calm and reduce irritation caused by excessive sweating, making it ideal for sensitive skin.

Astringent and drying effects: Maidenhair tree cream has astringent and drying properties that help reduce sweat production and control moisture levels on the skin, providing long-lasting relief from excessive sweating.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Ginkgo biloba, also known as maidenhair tree, creams helps with excessive sweating because of its unique combination of antioxidants and flavonoids. These active compounds work together to improve blood circulation, reduce inflammation, and regulate sweat gland function. As a result, Ginkgo biloba creams can help to reduce excessive sweating, also known as hyperhidrosis, and promote a sense of calm and well-being. Its natural properties make it an attractive alternative to harsh chemical-based treatments.
remedy_constituents: Flavonoids: These polyphenolic compounds help with excessive sweating by improving circulation, reducing inflammation, and modulating the body's temperature regulation, which can help alleviate sweating.

Terpenoids: Specifically, ginkgolides and bilobalide, which are terpenoid compounds found in Ginkgo biloba, help with excessive sweating by inhibiting the release of neurotransmitters that stimulate sweat gland activity, thus reducing sweat production.

Ginkgolic acids: These phenolic compounds have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may help with excessive sweating by reducing inflammation and oxidative stress in the body, leading to a decrease in sweat production.
remedy_parts: Leaves: The leaves of Ginkgo biloba are commonly used due to their high content of flavonoids and terpenoids, which have antiperspirant and anti-inflammatory properties.
Barks: The barks of Ginkgo biloba contain flavonoids and terpenoids that help reduce sweating by inhibiting the activity of sweat glands.

Seeds: The seeds of Ginkgo biloba are also used to make creams for excessive sweating due to their ability to inhibit the production of sweat by blocking the release of neurotransmitters that stimulate sweat glands.
remedy_recipe: Infuse 50g of dried ginkgo biloba leaves in 250ml of carrier oil such as sweet almond oil for 4-6 weeks.
Strain the infusion through cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a clean container to remove solids.
Combine 10g of beeswax and 20g of cocoa butter in a double boiler to melt the wax.
Mix the infused oil with the melted wax mixture in a ratio of 3:1 oil to wax.
Pour the mixture into small containers and allow to cool and harden for at least 30 minutes.

remedy_properties: Astringent properties: Geranium cream's astringent properties help to reduce sweat and body odor by constricting pores and drying up excess moisture.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Pelargonium graveolens, also known as geranium, creams helps with excessive sweating because of its unique properties. The essential oil extracted from this plant has natural antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties, which help to reduce bacterial growth and inflammation associated with excessive sweating. The oil's ability to balance the body's natural pH levels also contributes to its effectiveness in reducing sweat production. Additionally, the calming and soothing effects of geranium oil can help to reduce stress, a common trigger for excessive sweating. This makes geranium creams a popular and effective solution for those suffering from excessive sweating.
remedy_constituents: Linalool: It helps with excessive sweating by inhibiting the activity of sweat glands and reducing the production of sweat.
Geraniol: It helps with excessive sweating by possessing antifungal and antibacterial properties, which can help to control the growth of microorganisms that may contribute to excessive sweating.
Limonene: It helps with excessive sweating by possessing antiseptic and antifungal properties, which can help to reduce the risk of skin infections and promote a healthy skin environment.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Leaves are the most commonly used part of Pelargonium graveolens due to their high concentration of essential oils, particularly geraniol, which has antiperspirant and antibacterial properties.
Flowers: Flowers are also used as they contain similar essential oils as the leaves, contributing to their antiperspirant and deodorizing properties in creams for excessive sweating.
Stems: The stems of Pelargonium graveolens can be used as a substitute for leaves or flowers, as they contain a smaller but still significant amount of essential oils, which can help reduce sweating.
remedy_recipe: Extract the essential oil from the plant by steam distillation for 2 hours at 100°c.
Mix 20% of the extracted essential oil with 80% of a base oil such as jojoba oil.
Heat the mixture gently in a double boiler at 40°c for 30 minutes to facilitate blending.
Add 1% of a natural emulsifier such as lecithin to the mixture and stir well for 10 minutes.
Pour the mixture into sterilized containers and allow it to cool and set at room temperature for 2 hours.

remedy_properties: Menthol: Menthol, a key compound in peppermint oil, helps to cool and soothe the skin, reducing sweat production by constricting blood vessels and blocking sweat glands.
Eucalyptol: Eucalyptol, another essential component of peppermint oil, has natural anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce redness and irritation caused by excessive sweating.
Tannins: Tannins present in peppermint cream may help to dry out the skin, reducing moisture levels and thus alleviating sweat-related issues like hyperhidrosis (excessive sweating).
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Mentha x piperita, also known as peppermint, creams helps with excessive sweating because of its natural cooling and decongestant properties. The menthol in peppermint creams works to temporarily reduce the body's temperature, thereby reducing sweat production. Additionally, the menthol helps to open up pores, allowing for better airflow and reducing moisture buildup. This can provide relief from excessive sweating, often associated with hyperhidrosis, and help to keep the skin feeling cooler and fresher. Regular use may also help to reduce sweat production over time.
remedy_constituents: Menthol: Menthol is a terpene that cools the skin, reducing the sensation of heat and thereby decreasing sweat production.
Limonene: Limonene is a terpene that helps to reduce inflammation and modulate the body's response to stress, which can contribute to excessive sweating.

Rosmarinic Acid: Rosmarinic acid is a phenolic compound that has anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which can help to soothe and calm the skin, reducing sweat production and alleviating excessive sweating symptoms.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Known for their cooling and antiperspirant properties, which help to reduce sweating and provide relief from excessive sweating.
Stems: Used for their menthol content, which acts as a natural coolant and helps to reduce sweating and body temperature.
Buds: Contain menthol and other compounds that help to cool the skin, reduce sweating, and provide relief from excessive sweating.
remedy_recipe: Infuse 250ml distilled water with 1 bunch of fresh leaves and 2 stalks of stems for 10 minutes.
Strain the liquid through a cheesecloth into a clean glass container to remove solids immediately.
Combine the liquid with 100g beeswax, 50g coconut oil, and 20g shea butter in a double boiler.
Heat the mixture over low heat, stirring occasionally, until the beeswax is fully melted in 20 minutes.
Remove from heat, let cool, then whip the mixture with a hand mixer until smooth and creamy in 10 minutes.

remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Pot marigold cream helps reduce inflammation, which can contribute to excessive sweating by soothing and calming the skin, thereby reducing sweat gland activity.

Antibacterial and antifungal properties: The cream's antimicrobial properties help prevent bacterial and fungal infections that can exacerbate excessive sweating by creating a conducive environment for microbial growth.

Antioxidant properties: Pot marigold's antioxidants neutralize free radicals that can damage skin cells, leading to increased sweat production; the antioxidant properties also help soothe and calm the skin.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Calendula officinalis, also known as pot marigold, creams helps with excessive sweating because of its soothing and antiseptic properties. The anti-inflammatory compounds present in Calendula officinalis reduce redness and irritation associated with excessive sweating, promoting a healthy skin environment. Additionally, its antimicrobial properties prevent the growth of bacteria that can cause body odor and further irritate the skin, creating a more balanced and comfortable skin condition. As a result, Calendula officinalis creams can provide effective relief from excessive sweating.
remedy_constituents: Calendulin: It helps with excessive sweating by reducing inflammation and soothing the skin, which may contribute to alleviating symptoms of excessive sweating.
Nuciferine: It helps by having a mild anticholinergic effect, which may help to decrease sweat production and alleviate excessive sweating symptoms.

Iridoid glycosides: They help by reducing inflammation and promoting the healing of damaged skin, which can be beneficial in alleviating excessive sweating caused by skin irritation or other underlying conditions.
remedy_parts: Flowers: They are the primary used part due to their high concentration of sesquiterpene lactones, which have anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties.
Leaves: They are utilized for their astringent and antiseptic properties, which help reduce sweat and prevent bacterial growth.
Seeds : Although less commonly used, seeds have been employed for their antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties, often in combination with other plant parts.
remedy_recipe: Infuse 30g of dried calendula flowers in 500ml of carrier oil such as sweet almond oil.
Strain the infused oil through a cheesecloth into a clean glass container after 2 weeks.
Combine 100g of distilled water with 20g of beeswax and 10g of vitamin e oil in a double boiler.
Gradually add the infused calendula oil to the melted wax mixture stirring constantly for 10 minutes.
Pour the cooled cream mixture into a clean glass jar and store in the refrigerator for up to 6 months.

remedy_properties: Antiperspirant property: Sandalwood contains a compound called beta-santalol, which helps to reduce sweat production and prevent sweat from reaching the skin's surface, thereby reducing body odor.
Anti-inflammatory property: Sandalwood has anti-inflammatory properties that help to soothe and calm the skin, reducing redness and irritation caused by excessive sweating.

Antimicrobial property: Sandalwood contains antimicrobial compounds like cinnamic acid and terpenoids that inhibit the growth of bacteria and fungi on the skin, reducing the occurrence of sweat-related infections and body odor.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Santalum album, also known as sandalwood, creams helps with excessive sweating because of its unique composition. Sandalwood oil contains alpha-santalol, a sesquiterpene with natural antiperspirant properties. This essential oil works by inhibiting the activity of apocrine glands, which are responsible for excessive sweating. Additionally, sandalwood has a cooling effect on the skin, providing relief from heat and discomfort associated with excessive sweating. By regulating body temperature and reducing sweat production, sandalwood creams offer a natural and soothing solution for individuals suffering from excessive sweating.
remedy_constituents: Santalol: Santalol, a sesquiterpene alcohol, helps reduce excessive sweating by inhibiting the activity of sweat glands and reducing the production of sweat.
Santene: Santene, a sesquiterpene, exhibits a cooling effect on the skin, which can help alleviate excessive sweating by creating a sense of coolness and reducing the sensation of heat.
β-Santalene: β-Santalene, a sesquiterpene, has been shown to have a deodorizing effect, which can help reduce body odor associated with excessive sweating.
remedy_parts: Barks: They contain santalol, a key compound that helps to reduce sweating and alleviate symptoms of excessive sweating.
Rhyzomes: They are rich in santalol, which has antiperspirant properties that help to control excessive sweating.
Roots: They contain a mixture of sesquiterpenes and other compounds that help to reduce sweating and provide relief for people with hyperhidrosis.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 2 cups of dried santalum album flowers and leaves by carefully cutting them from mature trees in the early morning.
Steam distill the harvested botanicals using a high-quality distillation apparatus for 4 hours to obtain essential oil.
Combine 1/2 cup of beeswax and 1/4 cup of shea butter in a double boiler and melt them together at 160°f.
Add 10ml of the santalum album essential oil to the melted beeswax and shea butter mixture and stir well.
Pour the mixture into a clean glass container and allow it to cool and solidify for 2 hours.

remedy_properties: Saponins: Saponins have been shown to reduce sweat production by inhibiting the activity of the eccrine glands, which are responsible for producing most of the body's sweat.

Flavonoids: Flavonoids, particularly hesperidin and naringenin, possess anti-inflammatory properties that help soothe skin irritation caused by excessive sweating, reducing the risk of infection and promoting healthy skin.

Citrus flavones: Citrus flavones, such as limonene and sinensetin, have been found to exhibit antimicrobial properties, which can help prevent bacterial overgrowth on the skin that can exacerbate sweat-related issues.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Citrus x aurantium, also known as bitter orange, creams helps with excessive sweating because of its natural properties that help reduce body temperature. The primary compound responsible, synephrine, acts as a vasoconstrictor, causing blood vessels to constrict and reducing blood flow to the skin's surface. This reduction in blood flow helps to decrease sweat production, providing relief from excessive sweating. Additionally, the citrus scent in these creams can also have a psychological effect, helping to reduce anxiety and stress, which can contribute to excessive sweating.
remedy_constituents: Synephrine: A synthetic analogue of ephedrine, synephrine helps reduce excessive sweating by constricting blood vessels and increasing sympathetic nervous system activity, which can help alleviate sweating caused by hyperhidrosis.
Vitamin C (Ascorbic Acid): As an antioxidant and a natural astringent, vitamin C helps reduce excessive sweating by tightening skin pores, improving skin elasticity, and preventing moisture loss.

Flavonoids (specifically, Naringenin): Flavonoids like naringenin, a flavanone found in citrus fruits, have a mild diuretic effect, which can help reduce body temperature and alleviate excessive sweating caused by heat-related conditions.
remedy_parts: Seeds: The seeds of Citrus x aurantium are used to make creams for excessive sweating due to their high essential oil content, particularly bergamot oil, which has antiseptic and deodorant properties.
Barks: The barks of Citrus x aurantium are used to make creams for excessive sweating due to their ability to inhibit the growth of bacteria and fungi that cause sweat odor.

Leaves: The leaves of Citrus x aurantium are used to make creams for excessive sweating due to their antiseptic and antimicrobial properties, which help to reduce sweat odor and prevent skin irritation.
remedy_recipe: Combine 10 grams of dried citrus x aurantium peel with 100 grams of jojoba oil in a clean glass bowl.
Heat the mixture gently over low heat for 1 hour to release the essential oils.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth into a clean glass container to remove solids.
Add 20 grams of beeswax and 10 grams of shea butter to the mixture and stir until melted.
Allow the mixture to cool and solidify before whipping it into a smooth and creamy consistency.

remedy_properties: Antimicrobial properties: Lemon balm's antimicrobial properties help to reduce the growth of bacteria and fungi on the skin, which can contribute to sweat-related issues like body odor and infections.

Anti-inflammatory properties: The anti-inflammatory compounds in lemon balm cream can help to reduce redness and irritation caused by excessive sweating, providing relief from discomfort and itchiness.
Astringent properties: Lemon balm's astringent properties can help to constrict the pores on the skin, reducing sweat production and giving the appearance of clearer, less oily skin.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Melissa officinalis, also known as lemon balm, creams helps with excessive sweating because of its unique ability to reduce stress and anxiety. The calming properties of Melissa officinalis soothe the nervous system, which in turn helps to regulate the body's temperature and sweat production. Additionally, its antiseptic and antimicrobial properties help to prevent bacterial growth and promote a healthy skin environment, reducing the occurrence of excessive sweating and related issues. This natural remedy provides relief from excessive sweating without harsh chemicals.
remedy_constituents: Linalool: Linalool, a terpene found in Melissa officinalis, has been shown to have a cooling effect on the skin, which helps to reduce excessive sweating.

Rosmarinic acid: Rosmarinic acid, a phenolic compound in Melissa officinalis, possesses anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which can help to soothe and calm the skin, reducing excessive sweat production.
Apigenin: Apigenin, a flavonoid found in Melissa officinalis, has been found to have a sedative effect on the nervous system, which can help to reduce anxiety-related excessive sweating.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Essential oils extracted from the leaves of Melissa officinalis are commonly used to make creams for excessive sweating due to their antiperspirant and antiseptic properties.
Leaves: The leaves are also used to make Melissa officinalis creams for excessive sweating because they contain compounds that help to reduce sweat production and prevent body odor.

Leaves: Melissa officinalis creams made from the leaves are also popular for treating excessive sweating because the leaves have natural astringent properties that help to tighten the skin and reduce sweat glands activity.
remedy_recipe: Harvest melissa officinalis leaves by pinching or cutting them from the plant when they are in full bloom and fragrant.
Dry the leaves in a warm place or with a dehydrator at 35-40°c for 1-2 hours to prevent spoilage.
Steep 20g of dried melissa officinalis leaves in 100ml of carrier oil such as sweet almond oil for 2-3 weeks.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a clean glass container to remove solids.
Mix 10ml of the infused oil with 5g of beeswax and 10ml of distilled water to create a smooth creamy texture.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Chamomile creams helps with excessive sweating because of its natural anti-inflammatory and antispasmodic properties. The cream calms and soothes the skin, reducing irritation and inflammation caused by excessive sweat. Additionally, chamomile's natural cooling effect helps to regulate body temperature, minimizing the sensation of overheating and sweating. As a result, herbal chamomile creams provide long-lasting relief from excessive sweating, promoting a sense of comfort and calmness for those who struggle with this common condition.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Chamomile's anti-inflammatory compounds, such as apigenin and luteolin, help reduce inflammation and swelling associated with excessive sweating, providing relief from discomfort and skin irritation.

Antioxidant properties: The antioxidant capabilities of chamomile cream help neutralize free radicals that can contribute to oxidative stress and damage caused by excessive sweating, promoting healthy skin and reducing the appearance of sweat stains and marks.

Calming and soothing properties: Chamomile's calming and soothing effects on the skin can help reduce anxiety and stress-related sweating, while also providing a comforting and relaxing sensation on the skin, making it an effective treatment for hot flashes and night sweats.
remedy_parts: Roots: Chamomile roots are a natural antiperspirant and anti-inflammatory, making them effective in reducing sweat and soothing irritated skin.
Barks: The barks of the chamomile plant contain apigenin, an antioxidant that helps to calm and regulate the nervous system, which can contribute to excessive sweating.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 20 grams of dried matricaria chamomilla flowers and grind them into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle.
Combine the ground chamomile powder with 100 grams of coconut oil in a double boiler set over boiling water.
Stir the mixture for 30 minutes, allowing it to infuse and blend together seamlessly without heat.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or a fine-mesh sieve into a clean glass bowl, discarding any solids that remain.
Allow the cream to cool and solidify at room temperature before transferring it to an air-tight container for storage and use.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Patchouli creams helps with excessive sweating because they contain antiperspirant properties that help to reduce moisture production. The herb patchouli has natural astringent and anti-inflammatory properties that work together to dry up sweat glands, reducing the amount of sweat produced. Additionally, the cream's emollient properties help to soothe and calm irritated skin, providing long-lasting relief from excessive sweating.
remedy_properties: Antimicrobial properties: Patchouli oil's antimicrobial properties help to combat bacteria and fungi that can thrive in sweaty environments, reducing the risk of infection and unpleasant odors.

Antioxidant properties: The antioxidants present in patchouli cream help to neutralize free radicals that can damage skin cells and contribute to excessive sweating, promoting overall skin health and resilience.

Astringent and anti-inflammatory properties: Patchouli's astringent and anti-inflammatory properties help to reduce sweat production by constricting pores and soothing irritated skin, while also reducing inflammation caused by prolonged sweating.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in antioxidants and antibacterial properties, patchouli roots help reduce sweat and prevent bacterial growth that can cause body odor.
Leaves: The astringent and anti-inflammatory properties of patchouli leaves help constrict sweat glands, reducing excessive sweating and promoting dryness.
remedy_recipe: Harvest high-quality pogostemon cablin leaves and clean them thoroughly with distilled water.
Steep one tablespoon of dried pogostemon cablin leaves in two cups of boiling water for ten minutes.
Combine the infused liquid with two tablespoons of coconut oil and one teaspoon of beeswax and heat gently until melted.
Allow the mixture to cool and thicken at room temperature for about twenty minutes.
Stir well and transfer the herbal cream into sterilized glass jars for immediate use.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Yarrow creams helps with excessive sweating because they contain the natural properties of the herb Achillea millefolium, which has a long history of use in traditional medicine. The cream absorbs quickly and easily into the skin, where it works to reduce sweat production by balancing the body's natural moisture levels. Yarrow's antiperspirant effects also help to dry up sweat glands, leaving the skin feeling refreshed, clean, and dry for extended periods.
remedy_properties: Antiseptic and anti-inflammatory compounds: Yarrow's antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties help soothe and calm the skin, reducing redness and irritation caused by excessive sweating.

Astringent and antimicrobial agents: The astringent and antimicrobial properties of yarrow help to constrict sweat glands, reduce sweat production, and prevent bacterial growth, which can exacerbate sweaty conditions like hyperhidrosis.

Antioxidant and anti-spasmodic compounds: Yarrow's antioxidant and anti-spasmodic properties help to neutralize free radicals that can contribute to excessive sweating and relax muscle spasms in the sweat glands, reducing their activity.
remedy_parts: Roots: Containing a high concentration of saponins, yarrow roots have anti-inflammatory properties that help soothe and calm sweaty skin.
Buds: Rich in flavonoids and phenolic acids, yarrow buds provide antioxidant benefits that reduce oxidative stress and promote healthy skin.
remedy_recipe: Harvest dried achillea millefolium leaves and flowers in quantities of 20 grams.
Combine harvested herbs with sweet almond oil and beeswax in a double boiler.
Heat the mixture at 160 degrees fahrenheit for 30 minutes to infuse herbal properties.
Strain the infused mixture through cheesecloth and discard solids in preparation for cream-making.
Whip the remaining liquid with coconut milk and vitamin e oil before pouring into sterilized jars.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Thyme creams helps with excessive sweating because they contain antimicrobial properties that help to reduce bacteria growth, which is a common cause of sweat odor. Additionally, thyme has natural astringent properties that can help to constrict pores and reduce the amount of sweat produced by the body. This can provide long-lasting relief from excessive sweating, helping to keep you feeling dry and confident throughout the day. Thyme creams can be used on specific areas prone to sweating, such as the armpits or feet, for effective results.
remedy_properties: Antimicrobial properties: Thyme's antimicrobial properties help to combat bacteria and fungi that thrive in moist environments, reducing the growth of microorganisms that contribute to sweat-related odor and infections.

Anti-inflammatory properties: Thyme's anti-inflammatory compounds, such as thymol and carvacrol, help to soothe and calm irritated skin, reducing redness and inflammation associated with excessive sweating.

Antiperspirant and astringent properties: Thyme's antiperspirant and astringent properties help to constrict sweat glands, reducing the amount of sweat produced and helping to dry up excessive moisture on the skin.
remedy_parts: Roots: Thyme roots have anti-inflammatory properties, which help soothe and calm the skin affected by excessive sweating.
Leaves: The leaves of the thyme plant contain essential oils that provide antibacterial and antifungal benefits, reducing bacterial growth associated with sweat odor.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 30g of fresh thymus vulgaris leaves and dry them in the shade for 24 hours.
Grind 15g of dried thymus vulgaris leaves into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle.
Combine 10ml of distilled water and 5g of beeswax in a double boiler and heat until melted.
Add the ground thymus vulgaris powder to the melted mixture and stir until well combined for 5 minutes.
Pour the mixture into sterilized glass jars and let it cool and solidify at room temperature.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Rosemary creams helps with excessive sweating because of its unique ability to stimulate the scalp's natural oil production, which in turn reduces sweat glands' activity. The aromatic properties of rosemary also help to calm the nervous system, alleviating anxiety and stress-related sweating triggers. Additionally, the anti-inflammatory compounds present in rosemary may help to reduce inflammation caused by excessive sweating, providing a soothing and calming effect on the skin.
remedy_properties: Antiperspirant properties: Rosemary contains compounds like camphor, bornyl acetate, and borneol that have been shown to reduce sweat glands' activity and thereby decrease sweat production.

Anti-inflammatory effects: Rosemary's antioxidants and volatile oils have anti-inflammatory properties that help soothe and calm the skin, reducing redness, irritation, and discomfort caused by excessive sweating.

Antibacterial and antifungal properties: Rosemary's essential oils have antibacterial and antifungal properties that prevent the growth of bacteria and fungi on the skin, which can cause body odor and further exacerbate excessive sweating.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rosemary roots contain a high concentration of antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds, which help to soothe and calm the skin affected by excessive sweating.
Bark: The bark of rosemary contains tannins, which have antiseptic and antibacterial properties, making it effective in reducing bacterial growth that can cause sweat-related infections.
remedy_recipe: Gather 20 grams of dried rosmarinus officinalis leaves and crush them into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle for about 10 minutes.
Combine the crushed rosemary powder with 100 milliliters of cold-pressed coconut oil in a double boiler over low heat for 30 minutes.
Stir in 20 grams of beeswax pellets and 5 grams of shea butter into the rosemary-infused oil mixture until fully dissolved after 10 minutes.
Remove the mixture from heat and let it cool to room temperature before whipping it with a hand mixer for about 5 minutes until smooth and creamy.
Transfer the herbal cream to sterilized containers, label them, and store in an airtight container at room temperature for up to six months or in the refrigerator for up to one year.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Tasmanian blue gum creams helps with excessive sweating because of its unique properties. The cream contains a concentrated extract of eucalyptus globulus, which has natural antiperspirant and antibacterial effects. When applied topically, it helps to reduce sweat production by soothing the skin and calming the nerves that stimulate sweating. Additionally, the cream's antioxidant and anti-inflammatory compounds help to minimize redness and irritation caused by excessive sweating, promoting a smooth and healthy complexion. As a result, Tasmanian blue gum creams provide effective relief for those struggling with hyperhidrosis.
remedy_properties: Antibacterial Properties: Tasmanian Blue Gum contains compounds like eucalyptol and caryophyllene, which have antibacterial properties that help combat bacteria that thrive in sweaty environments, reducing the risk of infection.

Anti-Inflammatory Properties: The cream's anti-inflammatory agents, such as flavonoids and terpenes, help reduce redness, swelling, and discomfort associated with excessive sweating, promoting a faster recovery process.

Sweat-Reducing and Deodorizing Properties: Tasmanian Blue Gum contains compounds like cineole and alpha-pinene, which have natural sweat-reducing and deodorizing properties that help control body odor and moisture levels.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the Tasmanian Blue Gum contain antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds that help soothe and calm the skin, reducing sweat and discomfort associated with excessive sweating.
Barks: The barks of the Tasmanian Blue Gum have astringent properties that help tighten pores and reduce sweat glands' activity, making it an effective ingredient for controlling excessive sweating.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh eucalyptus globulus leaves in the early morning for optimal oil content and quality.
Dry the harvested leaves using shade drying or air circulation at room temperature for 7-10 days.
Combine 250g of dried eucalyptus globulus leaves with 500ml of carrier oil such as coconut or olive oil in a heat-proof glass container.
Simmer the mixture over low heat for 2 hours, straining and discarding solids before transferring to an amber glass bottle.
Allow the infused oil to cool and mature at room temperature for 24-48 hours before whipping with beeswax and essential oils as needed.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Ginger creams helps with excessive sweating because of its natural anti-inflammatory and antiperspirant properties. The active compounds in ginger, such as gingerol and shogaol, help to reduce sweat gland activity and alleviate symptoms of hyperhidrosis. Additionally, the cream's moisturizing effects soothe and calm the skin, reducing redness and irritation caused by excessive sweating. This natural remedy provides a safe and effective solution for those seeking relief from excessive sweating without harsh chemicals or pharmaceuticals.
remedy_properties: Antimicrobial properties: Ginger cream's antimicrobial agents help to inhibit the growth of bacteria on the skin, reducing sweat odor and preventing infections caused by excessive sweating.

Anti-inflammatory properties: Ginger's anti-inflammatory compounds, such as gingerols and shogaols, reduce inflammation and soothe irritated skin caused by excessive sweating, promoting a dry and comfortable skin tone.

Sudorific and antiperspirant properties: Ginger's natural sudorific properties help to stimulate sweat glands, allowing for more effective detoxification of the body through perspiration, while its antiperspirant compounds help to reduce sweat production.
remedy_parts: Rhyzomes: Rhyzomes are the underground stems of the ginger plant, which have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that help reduce sweat and soothe skin irritation.
Roots: Ginger roots contain compounds like salicylic acid, which has been shown to have antiperspirant properties and can help reduce excessive sweating.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh ginger root, wash it thoroughly, and peel off the skin before chopping into small pieces for infusion.
Combine 50 grams of chopped ginger with 500 milliliters of boiling water in a large glass bowl, allowing it to steep for 10 minutes.
Strain the infused ginger liquid through a cheesecloth or a fine-mesh sieve into another container, discarding the solids and setting aside the liquid for use.
Mix one part of the strained ginger liquid with three parts of cold-pressed coconut oil in a double boiler, heating it gently over low heat until fully incorporated and warm to the touch.
Remove the mixture from the double boiler, allowing it to cool and thicken slightly at room temperature before transferring it to an airtight container for storage and future use.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Fennel creams helps with excessive sweating because of its natural ability to reduce sweat production. The herb fennel has a cooling effect on the skin, which helps to regulate body temperature and minimize perspiration. Additionally, fennel's anti-inflammatory properties soothe and calm the skin, reducing irritation and discomfort associated with excessive sweating. By applying a fennel-based cream or lotion to affected areas, individuals can experience a significant reduction in sweat production and improved overall comfort.
remedy_properties: Antimicrobial properties: Fennel's antimicrobial compounds, such as cinnamic acid and camphor, help combat bacteria and fungi that contribute to body odor and sweat buildup, reducing the appearance of sweat stains and unpleasant smells.
Astringent properties: Fennel's astringent compounds, including volatile oils and flavonoids, help dry up excessive sweat and shrink pores, making skin appear less oily and more even-toned.

Cooling and anti-inflammatory properties: Fennel's cooling properties, combined with its anti-inflammatory compounds, help soothe and calm irritated skin, reducing redness and discomfort associated with excessive sweating.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots are used to create a cooling effect, helping to reduce sweat and alleviate associated discomfort.
Seeds: Fennel seeds have natural antiperspirant properties, making them effective in reducing sweat and odor.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh foeniculum vulgare leaves and stems in the early morning after dew has evaporated.
Dry the harvested plant material using a food dehydrator at 55°c for 24 hours or air drying for 7 days.
Combine 20% dried foeniculum vulgare with 80% coconut oil in a double boiler and stir until mixture reaches 65°c.
Allow the mixture to cool to room temperature and then strain it through cheesecloth into a clean container.
Store the cream in an airtight container at room temperature for up to 6 months or refrigerate for up to 12 months.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Sage creams helps with excessive sweating because they contain salvanic acids, which have a drying effect on the skin. These acids help to reduce sweat production by blocking the nerve signals that stimulate sweating. Additionally, sage's antifungal and antibacterial properties can help to prevent infections caused by moist environments. As a result, sage creams can provide long-lasting relief from excessive sweating, making them an effective natural solution for individuals suffering from hyperhidrosis.
remedy_properties: Antiperspirant and anti-inflammatory properties: Sage's antioxidants and flavonoids help to reduce sweat production and soothe skin irritation caused by excessive sweating, allowing for a more comfortable and dry experience.
Astringent properties: The tannins present in sage help to constrict the pores, reducing moisture absorption and oil secretion, which can contribute to excessive sweating and body odor.
Antimicrobial properties: Sage's antibacterial and antifungal compounds inhibit the growth of bacteria and fungi that thrive in sweaty environments, preventing infections and body odor.
remedy_parts: Roots: Sage roots have anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, making them effective in reducing sweat and preventing bacterial growth.
Buds: Sage buds are rich in antioxidants and flavonoids, which help to soothe skin irritation and reduce inflammation caused by excessive sweating.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh salvia officinalis leaves measuring exactly 20 grams and rinse them thoroughly under cold running water.
Combine the harvested leaves with 200 milliliters of carrier oil such as sweet almond or coconut oil in a heat-resistant glass bowl.
Heat the mixture over low heat for precisely 30 minutes, stirring occasionally to facilitate even infusion and prevent scorching.
Strain the infused oil through a cheesecloth or fine-mesh sieve into a clean glass container discarding the solids and pressing on the solids gently.
Allow the oil to cool completely before whipping it with 100 grams of natural emulsifier like beeswax and 50 grams of shea butter until smooth and creamy texture is achieved.

intro_desc: Herbal creams for excessive sweating are topical products infused with natural ingredients that help reduce excessive sweating by inhibiting the production of sweat glands and soothing the skin. The best herbal creams for excessive sweating include Melaleuca alternifolia, Aloe barbadensis, Zingiber officinale, Glycyrrhiza glabra, Cymbopogon citratus, Eucalyptus globulus, Sesamum indicum, Aloe vera, Saponaria officinalis, and Lavandula angustifolia. If left untreated, excessive sweating can severely impact daily life, causing social anxiety, embarrassing situations, and skin irritation, ultimately affecting a person's self-esteem and overall well-being.
supplementary_causes: Ailments similar to excessive sweating that are treated with herbal creams are acne, eczema, and rosacea. Herbal creams containing ingredients like tea tree oil, aloe vera, and chamomile have been shown to reduce inflammation and soothe skin irritations associated with these conditions. These natural remedies can help alleviate symptoms such as redness, itching, and dryness, promoting healthy and clear skin.
supplementary_best_treatment: The best combination of herbal creams that help with excessive sweating is a blend of tea tree oil, peppermint oil, and aloe vera. Tea tree oil has antifungal and antibacterial properties that reduce body odor and prevent infection. Peppermint oil cools the skin and reduces sweat production. Aloe vera soothes and calms the skin, reducing inflammation and promoting healing. Applying a mixture of these three ingredients to the affected area can provide effective relief from excessive sweating and promote overall skin health. Regular use can lead to long-term benefits.

remedies.remedy_desc: Melaleuca alternifolia creams helps with excessive sweating because they contain tea tree oil, a natural antiseptic and anti-inflammatory agent. This oil has the ability to penetrate deep into the skin, reducing bacterial growth and inflammation that can contribute to excessive sweating. By minimizing these underlying causes, tea tree oil creams can help to control sweat production, making them an effective solution for individuals experiencing hyperhidrosis. Regular use of these creams can lead to improved skin health and reduced sweat levels.
remedy_constituents: Cineole: Helps reduce excessive sweating by inhibiting the nerve signals that stimulate sweat glands, thus providing a cooling effect.
Limonene: Exhibits antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties, which aid in soothing and calming the skin, thereby reducing excessive sweating.
Caryophyllene: Possesses anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which help to reduce skin irritation and discomfort associated with excessive sweating.
remedy_parts: Leaves: The leaves of Melaleuca alternifolia are the most commonly used part in creams for excessive sweating because of their high tea tree oil content, which has antifungal and antibacterial properties that help combat sweat-related issues.

Barks: The barks of Melaleuca alternifolia are used due to their ability to provide a cooling sensation and reduce inflammation, making them a popular choice for creams aimed at soothing excessive sweating.
Seeds are not commonly used, but Leaves and Barks can also be combined with Fruits to make creams, however I have only used Fruits in this list below:
Fruits: The fruits of Melaleuca alternifolia are used to enhance the moisturizing properties of the creams, helping to keep the skin hydrated and calm in cases of excessive sweating.
remedy_recipe: Weigh 200g of base cream and 30g of beeswax in a double boiler for melting.
Add 5g of melaleuca alternifolia essential oil to 30g of distilled water and stir well for 10 minutes.
Combine the melted beeswax mixture with the distilled water mixture and stir until smooth consistency is achieved.
Add 10g of vitamin e oil and 5g of aloe vera gel to the mixture and stir well for 5 minutes.
Pour the mixture into sterile containers and allow to cool and solidify for 30 minutes.

remedies.remedy_desc: Aloe barbadensis creams helps with excessive sweating because of its natural antiperspirant properties. The gel extracted from the Aloe vera plant contains compounds like aloe-emodin and aloin, which have been shown to reduce sweat production and alleviate hyperhidrosis. Additionally, Aloe vera's soothing and calming effects on the skin can help to reduce inflammation and irritation associated with excessive sweating. Its natural anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties also aid in promoting a healthy skin environment, making it an effective remedy for excessive sweating.
remedy_constituents: Chrysophanic acid: It has a cooling effect on the skin, reducing the sensation of heat and thereby minimizing excessive sweating.
Aloe-emodin: It possesses astringent properties, which help to constrict blood vessels and reduce sweat gland activity, thus alleviating excessive sweating.
Aloin: It acts as a mild diuretic, promoting the removal of excess fluids from the body and reducing the amount of sweat produced.
remedy_parts: Leaves: The gel from Aloe barbadensis leaves is used because of its anti-inflammatory and soothing properties, which help to calm the skin and reduce sweating.
Leaves: The juice from Aloe barbadensis leaves contains salicylic acid, which has antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties that help to reduce sweating and prevent infections.
Leaves: The extract from Aloe barbadensis leaves contains vitamins and minerals that help to regulate body temperature and reduce excessive sweating.
remedy_recipe: Gather 200 grams of aloe barbadensis gel, 100 grams of distilled water, and 50 grams of jojoba oil.
Weigh and combine aloe barbadensis gel, distilled water, and jojoba oil in a stainless steel mixing bowl.
Heat the mixture over low heat for 10 minutes, then remove from heat and let cool.
Add 20 grams of beeswax and 5 grams of vitamin e oil to the cooled mixture.
Stir the mixture thoroughly until it reaches a smooth, thick consistency and is ready to use.

remedies.remedy_desc: Zingiber officinale creams helps with excessive sweating because of its natural anti-inflammatory properties and ability to regulate body temperature. The active compounds in ginger, such as gingerol and shogaol, have been shown to inhibit the activity of the sweat glands, reducing excessive sweating. Additionally, the cooling sensation of ginger can help to calm the skin and reduce the production of sweat, providing a soothing and comforting relief from excessive sweating. This natural remedy offers a gentle and effective solution for those struggling with this condition.
remedy_constituents: Gingerols: These compounds have anti-inflammatory and antispasmodic properties, which help to reduce sweat gland activity and alleviate excessive sweating.
Shogaols: Similar to gingerols, shogaols exhibit antispasmodic and anti-inflammatory properties, which help to calm down the nervous system and decrease sweat production.
Curcumenol: This compound has a cooling effect on the skin, which helps to reduce heat and sweat production, thereby alleviating excessive sweating.
remedy_parts: Rhyzomes: They are rich in bioactive compounds that help to reduce sweat and alleviate symptoms of excessive sweating.
Roots: The roots of ginger contain anti-inflammatory properties that help to soothe and calm the skin, reducing sweat and discomfort.
Barks: The barks of ginger contain compounds that help to regulate body temperature and reduce sweat, providing relief from excessive sweating.
remedy_recipe: Combine 10 grams of dried zingiber officinale roots with 100 ml of carrier oil in a double boiler.
Heat the mixture at 60-70 degrees celsius for 2 hours to infuse the oil with herbal properties.
Strain the infused oil through a cheesecloth or fine mesh to remove the solids.
Mix 20 grams of beeswax with 50 grams of shea butter in a heat-proof bowl.
Blend the infused oil with the beeswax and shea butter mixture until a smooth cream forms.

remedies.remedy_desc: Glycyrrhiza glabra creams helps with excessive sweating because they contain licorice extract, which has anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The soothing and calming effects of licorice root can help reduce the production of sweat, promoting a balance in the body's natural cooling process. Additionally, the antimicrobial properties of Glycyrrhiza glabra can help combat the growth of bacteria that thrive in sweaty areas, further reducing the occurrence of excessive sweating. This natural approach can provide long-lasting relief from sweating.
remedy_constituents: Glycyrrhizin: It helps with excessive sweating by reducing inflammation and soothing the skin, which can help to calm down the sweat glands and reduce sweating.
Liquiritin: It has antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties, which can help to prevent bacterial growth and reduce skin irritation that can contribute to excessive sweating.
Glycyrrhetinic acid: It has astringent properties, which can help to constrict blood vessels and reduce sweating by minimizing the flow of blood to the sweat glands.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of Glycyrrhiza glabra are primarily used to make creams for excessive sweating due to their high concentration of glycyrrhizin, a compound with anti-inflammatory and antiperspirant properties.
Roots: The roots also contain licorice saponins, which have been shown to help reduce sweat production and soothe skin irritation.
Roots: The roots' triterpenoid content, particularly glycyrrhetinic acid, contributes to their ability to inhibit sweat gland activity and alleviate excessive sweating symptoms.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 500g of dried glycyrrhiza glabra roots and grind them into a fine powder for later use.
Combine the ground powder with 100g of coconut oil and 50g of beeswax in a double boiler.
Stir the mixture continuously over low heat for 20 minutes or until the mixture is fully melted.
Remove the double boiler from heat and let it cool for 10 minutes before adding 20g of shea butter.
Pour the mixture into a glass jar and let it cool and set for 30 minutes before use.

remedies.remedy_desc: Cymbopogon citratus creams helps with excessive sweating because they contain the natural oils and extracts of the lemongrass plant, which have potent antiperspirant and deodorizing properties. The active compounds in lemongrass oil, such as citral and geraniol, help to reduce sweat gland activity and inhibit the growth of bacteria that can cause body odor. By addressing the root causes of excessive sweating, lemongrass creams can provide effective relief and promote a fresher, more confident you. Regular use can lead to significant improvements in sweat reduction and body odor control.
remedy_constituents: Citronellol: Acts as an antiperspirant and helps to reduce sweat gland activity by inhibiting the release of sweat.
Limonene: Possesses astringent properties that help to reduce sweat and prevent bacterial growth on the skin, thus alleviating excessive sweating.
Geraniol: Exhibits antifungal properties that help to inhibit the growth of microorganisms that can contribute to excessive sweating, such as yeast and bacteria.
remedy_parts: Leaves: They are used due to their high content of citral, a natural deodorant and antiperspirant agent.
Rhyzomes: They contain essential oils that help to reduce sweat and body odor.
Leaves: They also have a cooling effect, which can provide relief from excessive sweating.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh cymbopogon citratus leaves in the morning when they are at their peak fragrance and oil content.
Dry the harvested leaves in a warm place for 7-10 days or until they are crispy and brittle.
Steep 100g of dried cymbopogon citratus in 500ml of carrier oil such as coconut oil for 2 weeks.
Strain the infused oil using cheesecloth or a coffee filter and discard the solids.
Mix 20% of the infused oil with 80% of a cream base and 0.5% of a preservative like phenonip.

remedies.remedy_desc: Eucalyptus globulus creams helps with excessive sweating because of its cooling and decongestant properties. The primary component, eucalyptol, has a natural ability to reduce sweat production by regulating body temperature. It also possesses antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties, which help to prevent bacterial growth and soothe irritated skin. As a result, individuals who apply Eucalyptus globulus creams may experience a significant reduction in excessive sweating, allowing them to feel more confident and comfortable in their daily lives. This natural remedy provides effective relief from hyperhidrosis.
remedy_constituents: Cineole (Eucalyptol): It helps with excessive sweating by acting as a natural decongestant, reducing sweat gland activity and promoting a sense of coolness and comfort.
Limonene: It helps with excessive sweating by reducing inflammation and promoting a feeling of dryness and comfort on the skin, thus providing relief from excessive sweating.
Caryophyllene: It helps with excessive sweating by inhibiting the production of sweat, thereby reducing excessive sweating and providing relief from associated skin irritations.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Eucalyptus globulus leaves are commonly used due to their high eucalyptol content, which has a cooling effect that helps reduce excessive sweating.
Barks: Eucalyptus globulus barks are used because of their antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties, which help soothe and calm the skin affected by excessive sweating.
Leaves: Eucalyptus globulus leaves are also used due to their decongestant properties, which help relieve the skin of excessive sweating and associated irritation.
remedy_recipe: Infuse 1 tablespoon of dried eucalyptus globulus leaves in 1 cup of boiling water for 5-7 minutes.
Strain the mixture and discard the solids then let it cool to room temperature.
Combine 1/4 cup of cooled infusion with 1/2 cup of distilled water and 1 tablespoon of emulsifying wax.
Melt the emulsifying wax in a double boiler and add 1 tablespoon of beeswax and 2 tablespoons of coconut oil.
Whisk the mixture until it thickens then pour it into a container and let it cool and solidify for 30 minutes.

remedies.remedy_desc: Sesamum indicum creams helps with excessive sweating because they contain sesamol and sesamin, natural compounds that have anti-inflammatory and antifungal properties. These compounds work to reduce the production of sweat glands, thereby minimizing excessive sweating. Additionally, sesamum indicum creams have a cooling effect on the skin, which helps to reduce the sensation of heat and discomfort associated with excessive sweating. This natural remedy provides a safe and effective solution for individuals seeking to manage hyperhidrosis.
remedy_constituents: Fatty acids: They help reduce sweat production by regulating body temperature and providing a cooling effect.
Phenolic acids: They have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which can help alleviate the underlying conditions that lead to excessive sweating.
Triterpenoids: They exhibit antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties, which can help soothe and protect the skin from irritation and infection caused by excessive sweating.
remedy_parts: Seeds: They are used due to their anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties, which help soothe and calm the skin.
Leaves: They are utilized for their cooling and antiperspirant properties, which help reduce sweat and alleviate symptoms of excessive sweating.
Seeds: They are also used for their antioxidant properties, which help protect the skin from damage caused by excessive sweating.
remedy_recipe: Extract 20 grams of sesamum indicum leaves in 50 milliliters of boiling distilled water for 10 minutes.
Strain the mixture through cheesecloth and collect the liquid for further processing.
Mix 10 grams of beeswax with 10 milliliters of the sesamum indicum liquid in a double boiler.
Heat the mixture at 60 to 70 degrees celsius for 10 to 15 minutes or until it thickens.
Cool the mixture and whip it with 5 milliliters of coconut oil to create a smooth cream.

remedies.remedy_desc: Aloe vera creams helps with excessive sweating because of its natural antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties. The gel extracted from the Aloe vera plant soothes and cools the skin, reducing irritation and discomfort caused by excessive sweating. Aloe vera's ability to balance skin pH helps to control sweat glands, while its anti-fungal properties prevent the growth of bacteria that can cause body odor. Regular application of Aloe vera cream can help to reduce sweat and leave the skin feeling cool and refreshed.
remedy_constituents: Aloe-emodin: This anthraquinone compound has anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which help reduce sweat and alleviate symptoms associated with excessive sweating.
Alkaloids (e.g., aloeine, aloin): These compounds have a cooling effect on the skin, reducing heat and sweat production, which can help alleviate excessive sweating.
Fatty acids (e.g., oleic acid, palmitic acid): These lipids have moisturizing properties, which help hydrate the skin and reduce sweat production, while also providing a soothing effect on the skin.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Aloe vera leaves are the primary source of gel, which is often used in creams to help reduce excessive sweating due to its ability to calm and soothe the skin.
Gel (derived from the Leaves): Aloe vera gel is used in creams to reduce sweat due to its cooling effect, anti-inflammatory properties, and ability to regulate the skin's temperature.
Glycerin (derived from the Leaves): Aloe vera's glycerin, extracted from its leaves, is commonly used in creams to help reduce excessive sweating by retaining moisture and regulating skin pH.
remedy_recipe: Gather 1 cup of aloe vera gel from mature leaves and 1/4 cup of coconut oil in a clean bowl.
Mix 1 tablespoon of beeswax and 2 tablespoons of shea butter with the aloe vera gel.
Combine 2 tablespoons of vitamin e oil and 1 teaspoon of essential oil with the mixture.
Heat the mixture over low heat for 10 minutes or until the beeswax is fully melted.
Pour the mixture into a clean container and refrigerate for 30 minutes to set the cream.

remedies.remedy_desc: Saponaria officinalis creams helps with excessive sweating because they contain natural saponins, which have been traditionally used to reduce perspiration. The herb's anti-inflammatory properties also soothe and calm the skin, providing relief from excessive sweating. Additionally, Saponaria officinalis has been found to have a mild antiseptic effect, which helps to prevent bacterial growth and infection in areas prone to excessive sweating, promoting a healthier and more balanced skin environment.
remedy_constituents: Saponins: These constituents help to reduce excessive sweating by their ability to dry the skin and reduce moisture levels, thus providing a soothing effect on hyperhidrosis-prone skin.

Phenolic acids (e.g., Salicylic acid): These compounds help to inhibit the activity of sweat glands, thereby reducing excessive sweating, and also exhibit anti-inflammatory properties that can soothe irritated skin.
Flavonoids (e.g., Quercetin): These constituents possess antioxidant properties that can help to reduce inflammation and soothe the skin, ultimately leading to a decrease in excessive sweating.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Used due to their ability to help reduce sweating and soothe skin irritations.
Flowers: Employed for their anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties to help combat excessive sweating.
Roots: Utilized for their astringent and antiseptic properties to help dry out and treat excessive sweating.
remedy_recipe: Infuse 20 grams of saponaria officinalis flowers in 100 ml of cold water for 2 hours at room temperature.
Strain the infused liquid through a cheesecloth or a fine-mesh sieve into a clean bowl.
Combine the infused liquid with 10 grams of beeswax and 20 grams of coconut oil in a double boiler.
Heat the mixture gently for 10 minutes or until the beeswax and coconut oil are fully incorporated.
Add 5 grams of shea butter and 5 grams of vitamin e oil to the mixture and whip until smooth.

remedies.remedy_desc: Lavandula angustifolia creams helps with excessive sweating because of its cooling and antiseptic properties. The active compounds in lavender oil, such as linalool and linalyl acetate, have a calming effect on the nervous system, which can help reduce anxiety and stress that may contribute to excessive sweating. Additionally, the antimicrobial properties of lavender oil can help prevent the growth of bacteria that can exacerbate sweat and body odor, promoting a fresher and healthier skin environment. This helps to alleviate excessive sweating symptoms naturally.
remedy_constituents: Linalool: Helps with excessive sweating by reducing stress and anxiety, which can contribute to excessive sweating, and also exhibits antimicrobial properties to prevent bacterial growth that can exacerbate sweat-related conditions.
Linalyl acetate: Helps with excessive sweating by its antispasmodic properties, which can help relax the sweat glands and reduce the frequency and intensity of sweating episodes.

Limonene: Helps with excessive sweating by its ability to reduce stress and anxiety, promote relaxation, and exhibit antimicrobial properties to prevent bacterial growth that can contribute to sweat-related conditions.
remedy_parts: Leaves: They are used due to their high content of linalool and linalyl acetate, which have antiseptic and antifungal properties that help control excessive sweating.
Flowers: They are used for their fragrance and therapeutic properties, including their ability to reduce stress and anxiety, which can be contributing factors to excessive sweating.
Stems: They are used due to their content of sesquiterpenes, which have anti-inflammatory properties that can help soothe and calm the skin affected by excessive sweating.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 1 pound of fresh lavandula angustifolia flowers and dry them completely in a well-ventilated area.
Infuse 1 cup of dried lavandula angustifolia flowers in 2 cups of carrier oil such as jojoba oil.
Combine 2 tablespoons of infused oil with 2 tablespoons of beeswax and 2 tablespoons of shea butter in a double boiler.
Heat the mixture in a double boiler at 160-180°f for 30 minutes while stirring occasionally to achieve a smooth consistency.
Pour the mixture into a glass jar and let it cool completely before applying it to the skin.
